title: [WIP] Investigate pixel comparer libraries for feature alignment
body:
- [x] **Enhance `SKPixelComparisonResult`** with standard metrics found in most image comparison libraries:
  - `SumSquaredError` (long) - raw sum of per-channel squared differences
  - `MeanAbsoluteError` (double) - average absolute error per channel per pixel  
  - `MeanSquaredError` (double) - standard per-channel MSE metric
  - `RootMeanSquaredError` (double) - RMSE (used by MAUI VisualTestUtils/MagickNet)
  - `NormalizedRootMeanSquaredError` (double) - RMSE / 255.0 (range 0-1, useful for threshold testing)
  - `PeakSignalToNoiseRatio` (double) - PSNR in dB (standard quality metric)
- [x] **Update core `Compare` methods** to track sum of squared errors
- [x] **Add tolerance-based `Compare` overloads** (like ImageMagick's "fuzz" threshold) for all input types: `Compare(first, second, int tolerance)` - per-pixel threshold without needing a heavy mask image
- [x] **Add `GenerateDifferenceImage` method** that produces actual per-channel difference visualization (not just binary B/W like current `GenerateDifferenceMask`)
- [x] **Add 22 comprehensive tests** for all new functionality
- [x] **Update pixel-comparer documentation** with new metrics, tolerance comparison, and difference image docs
- [x] Address code review feedback: use explicit constructor overloads for binary compatibility
- [x] Run security scanning (CodeQL: 0 alerts)

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---
